question: NH3 (g) was synthesized at 200°C in the presence of a powdered Os(s) catalyst, leading to the equilibrium system represented above. Which of the following changes would result in more NH3 in the mixture after equilibrium is reestablished?

A mixture of NO2(g) and N2O4(g) is placed in a glass tube and allowed to reach equilibrium at 70°C, as represented below. N2O4(g) ↔ 2 NO2(g) Kp = 3.0 at 70°C. If PN2O4 is 1.33 atm when the system is at equilibrium at 70°C what is PNO2?

N2O4(g) ↔ 2 NO2(g) Kp = 3.0 at 70°C. Which of the following best predicts how the partial pressures of the reacting species will be affected if a small amount of Ar(g) is added to the equilibrium mixture at constant volume?

responses.1: PNO2 will decrease and PN2O4 will increase.
responses.2: PNO2 will increase and PN2O4 will decrease.
responses.3: Both PNO2 and PN2O4 will decrease.

question: The equilibrium system represented by the equation below initially contains equal concentrations of Cr2O72-(aq) and CrO42-(aq). Cr2O72-(aq) + 3 H2O(l) ⇌ 2 CrO42-(aq) + 2 H3O+(aq). Orange Yellow. Which of the following statements correctly predicts the result of adding a sample of 6.0 M NaOH(aq) to the system, and provides an explanation?

responses.1: The mixture will become more orange because OH- will oxidize the Cr in CrO42-(aq).
responses.2: The mixture will become more yellow because OH- will reduce the Cr in Cr2O72-(aq).
responses.3: The mixture will become more yellow because OH- will shift the equilibrium toward the products.
responses.4: The color of the mixture will not change because OH- does not appear in the equilibrium expression.

question: 2 BaO2(s) ⇌ 2 BaO(s) + O2(g) ΔH° = 162 kJ/mol_rxn. A sealed rigid vessel contains BaO2(s) in equilibrium with BaO(s) and O2(g) as represented by the equation above. Which of the following changes will increase the amount of BaO2(s) in the vessel?

responses.1: Removing a small amount of O2(g)
responses.2: Removing a small amount of BaO(s)
responses.3: Adding He gas to the vessel

question: The synthesis of CH3OH from CO and H2 is represented by the equation below: CO + 2 H2 → CH3OH ΔH < 0. Which of the following statements is true about the bond energies in this reaction?

responses.1: The energy absorbed as the bonds in the reactants are broken is greater than the energy released as the bonds in the products are formed.
responses.2: The energy released as the bonds in the reactants are broken is greater than the energy absorbed as the bonds in the products are formed.
responses.3: The energy released as the bonds in the reactants are broken is less than the energy absorbed as the bonds in the products are formed.
responses.4: The energy absorbed as the bonds in the reactants are broken is less than the energy released as the bonds in the products are formed.

question: Given reactions and their equilibrium constants: FeF2(s) ⇌ Fe2+(aq) + 2 F-(aq) K1 = 2*10^-6. F-(aq) + H+(aq) ⇌ HF(aq) K2 = 1*10^3. FeF2(s) + 2 H+(aq) ⇌ Fe2+(aq) + 2 HF(aq) K3 = ?. Is the reaction represented by the third equation thermodynamically favored?

Ca(OH)2(s) ⇌ Ca2+(aq) + 2 OH-(aq) Ksp = 5.5*10^-6 at 298 K. The equilibrium in a saturated solution of Ca(OH)2 is represented above. In an experiment, a student places 5.0 g of Ca(OH)2(s) into 100.0 mL of distilled water and stirs the mixture. How would the results be affected if the student repeats the experiment but this time places 5.0 g of Ca(OH)2(s) into 100.0 mL of 0.0010 M NaOH(aq) instead of distilled water?

responses.1: Less solid will dissolve, because the larger value of [OH-] will cause the equilibrium position to lie farther to the right.
responses.2: Less solid will dissolve, because the larger value of [OH-] will cause the equilibrium position to lie farther to the left.
responses.3: More solid will dissolve, because the larger value of [OH-] will cause the equilibrium position to lie farther to the right.
responses.4: More solid will dissolve, because the smaller value of [OH-] will cause the equilibrium position to lie farther to the left.

question: PCl3(g) + Cl2(g) ⇌ PCl5(g) Kc = 6.5. At a certain point in time, a 1.00 L rigid reaction vessel contains 1.5 mol of PCl3(g), 1.0 mol of Cl2(g), and 2.5 mol of PCl5(g). Which of the following describes how the measured pressure in the reaction vessel will change and why it will change that way as the reaction system approaches equilibrium at constant temperature?

responses.1: The pressure will increase because Q < Kc
responses.2: The pressure will increase because Q > Kc
responses.3: The pressure will decrease because Q < Kc
responses.4: The pressure will decrease because Q > Kc

question: MANUAL UPLOAD NEEDED FROM UNIT 7 SET A Equimolar samples of Pb(OH)2(s), PbI2(s) and PbF2(s) are placed in three separate beakers, each containing 250 mL of water at 25°C. 2SO2(g) + O2(g) ⇌ 2SO3(g)
Which of the following is the correct result of the reaction provided above depending on the relationship of Q and K when O2 is being added?

responses.1: When O2 is being added when Q < K, SO3 will be produced more.
responses.2: When O2 is being added when Q > K, SO3 will be produced more.
responses.3: When O2 is being added when Q < K, SO2 will be produced more.
responses.4: When O2 is being added when Q > K, SO2 will be produced less.

question:
BaF2(s) ⇌ Ba2+(aq) + 2F-(aq)
The provided reaction is at equilibrium at a certain temperature during an experiment. Initially, the student performed the experiment with distilled water. During the second experiment, the student decided to use CaF2 instead of distilled water. Which of the following would result in the second experiment?

responses.1: Less ions will form, because the equilibrium will shift to the left.
responses.2: Less ions will form, because the equilibrium will shift to the right.
responses.3: More ions will form, because the equilibrium will shift to the left.
responses.4: More ions will form, because the equilibrium will shift to the right.
